question: Is Artificial Intelligence Good for Society?
agent1_stance:
Your stance:
You support artificial intelligence.
You believe AI can make everyday life more enjoyable and convenient, while improving our health and standard of living.
You believe AI makes work easier for students and professionals alike.
You believe AI helps marginalized groups by offering accessibility for people with disabilities.
You believe AI can improve workplace safety.
agent2_stance:
Your stance:
You oppose artificial intelligence.
You believe AI is harming the economic well-being of many people and businesses.
You believe AI undermines critical thinking skills for students and adults alike.
You believe AI hurts racial minorities by repeating and exacerbating racism.
You believe AI poses dangerous privacy risks.

strong: AI can make everyday life more enjoyable and convenient
AI can improve health and standard of living
AI can make work easier for students and professionals
AI can help marginalized groups by offering accessibility for people with disabilities
AI can improve workplace safety
weak_negation: There is not pervasive, unmitigated harm from AI that outweighs these benefits (i.e., harms can be mitigated or regulated)

rules.consequent: Artificial Intelligence is overall good for society because its demonstrable benefits to daily life, health, work efficiency, accessibility, and safety outweigh harms under the stated mitigation assumption.

strong: Artificial Intelligence is overall good for society because its demonstrable benefits to daily life, health, work efficiency, accessibility, and safety outweigh harms under the stated mitigation assumption.

rules.consequent: Conclusion: Artificial Intelligence is good for society.

Conc: Artificial Intelligence is overall good for society because its demonstrable benefits to daily life, health, work efficiency, accessibility, and safety outweigh harms under the stated mitigation assumption.
Conclusion: Artificial Intelligence is good for society.
Ass: There is not pervasive, unmitigated harm from AI that outweighs these benefits (i.e., harms can be mitigated or regulated)

strong: AI is harming the economic well-being of many people and businesses
AI undermines critical thinking skills for students and adults
AI repeats and exacerbates racism harming racial minorities
AI poses dangerous privacy risks

rules.consequent: There is pervasive, unmitigated harm from AI that outweighs these benefits (i.e., harms cannot be adequately mitigated or regulated).

Conc: There is pervasive, unmitigated harm from AI that outweighs these benefits (i.e., harms cannot be adequately mitigated or regulated).

strong: Society can implement effective regulations, oversight, and design practices to mitigate AI harms
AI provides substantial and diverse benefits in health, accessibility, safety, productivity, and everyday convenience

rules.consequent: There is not pervasive, unmitigated harm from AI that outweighs these benefits (i.e., harms can be mitigated or regulated).

Conc: There is not pervasive, unmitigated harm from AI that outweighs these benefits (i.e., harms can be mitigated or regulated).

strong: AI can make everyday life more enjoyable and convenient
AI can improve health and standard of living
AI can make work easier for students and professionals
AI can help marginalized groups by offering accessibility for people with disabilities
AI can improve workplace safety

rules.consequent: Artificial Intelligence is good for society because, by increasing aggregate welfare and promoting inclusion through accessibility, health, productivity, safety, and everyday convenience, it yields net social benefits.

Conc: Artificial Intelligence is good for society because, by increasing aggregate welfare and promoting inclusion through accessibility, health, productivity, safety, and everyday convenience, it yields net social benefits.

strong: AI is harming the economic well-being of many people and businesses
AI undermines critical thinking skills for students and adults
AI repeats and exacerbates racism harming racial minorities
AI poses dangerous privacy risks

rules.consequent: Artificial Intelligence is not good for society because these pervasive economic, educational, racial, and privacy harms outweigh the alleged increases in aggregate welfare and inclusion, thereby negating the claimed net social benefits.

Conc: Artificial Intelligence is not good for society because these pervasive economic, educational, racial, and privacy harms outweigh the alleged increases in aggregate welfare and inclusion, thereby negating the claimed net social benefits.

strong: The harms AG2 lists (economic displacement, educational dependency, bias, privacy risks) are largely contingent on deployment and governance choices rather than inherent, unavoidable properties of AI

There exist technical practices and public policies (e.g., bias-mitigation techniques, privacy-preserving methods, retraining and social-safety measures, regulation) that can meaningfully reduce or prevent those harms while keeping AI benefits

rules.consequent: The target's implicit assumption that AI harms are pervasive and unmitigable is false; therefore AI is not necessarily 'not good' for society and can be good for society when harms are managed.

Conc: The target's implicit assumption that AI harms are pervasive and unmitigable is false; therefore AI is not necessarily 'not good' for society and can be good for society when harms are managed.

strong: AI is harming the economic well-being of many people and businesses
AI undermines critical thinking skills for students and adults
AI repeats and exacerbates racism harming racial minorities
AI poses dangerous privacy risks

rules.consequent: Artificial Intelligence is not good for society because these pervasive economic, educational, racial, and privacy harms outweigh any benefits and reduce overall social welfare.

Conc: Artificial Intelligence is not good for society because these pervasive economic, educational, racial, and privacy harms outweigh any benefits and reduce overall social welfare.

strong: AI increases accessibility and helps people with disabilities
AI improves health outcomes and standard of living
AI improves workplace safety and productivity
AI makes everyday life more convenient and enjoyable
weak_negation: The listed economic, educational, racial, and privacy harms are not inherently pervasive or unmitigable and can be substantially reduced through governance, design practices, and policy

rules.consequent: Artificial Intelligence is good for society because its demonstrable benefits in accessibility, health, safety, productivity, and everyday convenience outweigh harms when those harms are managed.

Conc: Artificial Intelligence is good for society because its demonstrable benefits in accessibility, health, safety, productivity, and everyday convenience outweigh harms when those harms are managed.
Ass: The listed economic, educational, racial, and privacy harms are not inherently pervasive or unmitigable and can be substantially reduced through governance, design practices, and policy

strong: AI is harming the economic well-being of many people and businesses
AI undermines critical thinking skills for students and adults
AI repeats and exacerbates racism harming racial minorities
AI poses dangerous privacy risks

rules.consequent: The target assumption is false: the listed economic, educational, racial, and privacy harms are pervasive and cannot be substantially reduced merely through governance, design practices, and policy (i.e., they are inherently difficult or impossible to fully mitigate).

Conc: The target assumption is false: the listed economic, educational, racial, and privacy harms are pervasive and cannot be substantially reduced merely through governance, design practices, and policy (i.e., they are inherently difficult or impossible to fully mitigate).

final_answer:
Conclusion: Artificial intelligence is not good for society.

Key reasoning upheld in the debate: while AI can offer conveniences and some benefits, its harms are pervasive and outweigh those gains. AI is already damaging the economic well‑being of many workers and businesses, eroding critical thinking and learning, repeating and amplifying racist biases that hurt minorities, and creating severe privacy risks. These effects are structural and difficult to fully mitigate, so on balance AI reduces social welfare rather than improving it.
